findings.title: Automate contract → onboarding form → calendar booking via Zapier
findings.claim: The moment a client fills out a sales intake form, a fully automated chain fires: contract sent via e-signatures.io, welcome email triggered on signing, onboarding form delivered, and kickoff calendar link sent — all without manual intervention.
findings.what_they_do: Three sequential Zapier workflows: (1) Notion sales intake form submission → e-signatures.io contract creation with placeholder fields auto-populated; (2) contract signed → Gmail welcome email with onboarding form link; (3) onboarding form submitted → Gmail email with GoHighLevel calendar booking link. Appointment reminders sent at 24hr, 1hr, and 5min via GHL automation. All built in Notion with a Zapier Pro account ($29.99/mo) and e-signatures.io pay-per-use ($0.49/contract).
findings.vs_us: Palm has a form wizard onboarding flow for creators (§6) and auto-generated PDF contracts via Puppeteer, but no documented automated chain that sequences contract → onboarding form → kickoff booking without manual steps. The creator-side onboarding is thin; there is no client/brand-side onboarding OS equivalent documented in Palm's baseline.
findings.recommendation: Build the same three-Zap chain for Palm's creator onboarding: sales intake form submission (Airtable or a form) triggers contract PDF (already Puppeteer-built), signing triggers an onboarding packet with a kickoff Calendly/GHL link, and form completion triggers a Telegram or email alert to Josh. Low incremental cost given existing Puppeteer contract system.

sources.quote: e signatures.io, it's not a $30 a month type of subscription to send my contracts. It is you pay per contract sent
sales intake form to contract. So, that's what we want to do
contract signed. Send onboarding form. Perfect. I'm going to go back to my folder

findings.title: Run a structured Canva onboarding presentation to kill scope creep at kickoff
findings.claim: A formal onboarding call presentation — covering process steps, revision limits, invoice terms, cancellation policy, and 'how to win' — eliminates scope creep and buyer remorse by setting hard expectations before any work begins.
findings.what_they_do: Build a Canva slide deck covering: welcome/team intro, reiterate buying decision with case studies, step-by-step delivery process with timelines, revision policy (2 revisions, batch feedback in one email), invoicing terms, meeting cancellation policy (72hr notice), additional services pricing, and monthly strategy call cadence. Present this at the kickoff call before any strategy discussion. Use it as a live walk-through shared on screen. Embed the deck in the client's Notion client OS for future reference.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no documented onboarding call SOP or expectation-setting presentation for creators (§6 is thin). The creator onboarding form wizard collects info but there is no structured kickoff call protocol. This gap means scope creep and misaligned expectations are likely at creator onboarding.
findings.recommendation: Create a Palm creator onboarding deck covering: what Palm does (social + content pipeline), creator responsibilities (filming, approvals), commission split and invoice cadence, content approval process and turnaround times, and escalation path. Embed in the creator-facing section of the admin portal or deliver via a recorded Loom. Assign to Josh or a future creator success role.

sources.quote: any frustration is just an unmet expectation. And if we're not doing a good job at setting expectations, then the client's going to be frustrated
I like to use Canva to build this out. So, if I'm on the client call... we're just going to go over a quick onboarding presentation just to make sure we're really aligned on expectations
revisions. If you do have revisions, super happy to help. We limit those to two

findings.topic: Notion-based fulfillment OS with per-role task board views
findings.title: Build role-filtered Notion task boards so each team member sees only their queue
findings.claim: A single Notion database for all fulfillment tasks, filtered into separate board views per role (videographer, editor, social media manager, CSM, strategist), eliminates cross-team confusion and missed deadlines without requiring multiple databases.
findings.what_they_do: One 'actions' Notion database with status stages (ready to script → scripting → ready to shoot → ready to edit → editing → ready to review → ready to send to client → sent to client → client approved → ready to post → done). Project templates pre-populate task lists (e.g., 8 videos + 3 ads for flagship offer). Each role gets a filtered view showing only their relevant statuses: videographer sees shoot-date calendar; editor sees editing dates and a countdown timer; CSM sees 'ready for review' and 'ready to send to client'; social media manager sees post-date calendar. Internal comments replace Slack/iMessage for revision feedback.
findings.vs_us: Palm has a mature 8-step content pipeline (§1) with Assets and Tasks tables in Airtable, an editor workflow with day-counter task queue, and 2-slot-per-day scheduling. This is structurally similar. Key gap: Palm's documented editor workflow covers submission→library→grid planner but does not describe per-role filtered views or cross-role handoff statuses within a single task record. The Notion approach adds a unified card that travels through all roles, keeping script, shoot footage, and edit all on one record.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate whether Palm's Airtable Tasks table can be extended with status stages and per-role filtered views (editor view, social media manager view, creator view) rather than rebuilding in Notion. The specific add: attach raw footage links, captions, and revision comments directly to the task card so editors and Amin don't need Slack/Telegram for task-level communication. This is a concrete improvement to the existing mature system.

sources.quote: we're going to assign owners at each stages so that it's really really clear
we created a board for that. So, we're going to go to video editor board and boom, ready to edit
the comment. I'm not communicating via Slack. I'm not communicating via iMessage. It's right here.

findings.topic: Monthly NPS survey system with action protocol
findings.title: Send a monthly NPS form to every active client and require a response to every score
findings.claim: Sending a net promoter score survey to all clients on the first of every month, routing responses into a tracked database, and requiring the CSM to respond to every score — including passives and detractors — directly improves retention and provides data to improve the product.
findings.what_they_do: Notion form (NPS survey) with name, email, score (0–10), and open comment. Repeating task on the 1st of each month assigned to CSM with the form link and a pre-written email template embedded in the task. On submission: Notion automation sends internal team email alert and a thank-you email to the client. Scores roll up to the client OS (average NPS per client visible at a glance). CSM board shows unresponded scores filtered by 'responded = unchecked.' Company-wide average NPS tracked on client success dashboard. Threshold: any score below 8 triggers immediate action review.
findings.vs_us: Palm has a Fan Tracker CRM and whale-hunting tools (§9) for fan retention, but no documented NPS or satisfaction survey system for creator retention. The §9 gap notes no written retention/churn/win-back strategy. This tactic maps directly to that gap — applied to Palm's creators rather than brand clients.
findings.recommendation: Implement a monthly creator satisfaction pulse (can be a simple Airtable form or Typeform): 1–10 score, one open question ('what could we do better?'), and one win-capture question ('any recent milestone we should celebrate?'). Route responses to Airtable, auto-alert Josh on any score below 7, and have a 48hr response SOP. This costs near-zero and directly addresses the §9 retention gap.

sources.quote: NPS. It's called net promoter score. Detractors, passives, promoters. We want to set up a system where we can send this out once per month
we don't want somebody to fill out an NPS form and then we don't respond
if this is below 8, below 9, I need to focus on this. I need to like dial this in like really really hardcore

findings.topic: Client wins database as retention and sales ammo
findings.title: Maintain a running 'wins database' — screenshots, metrics, clips — per client for retention and resigning
findings.claim: Systematically capturing and storing client wins (lead screenshots, view counts, clips) in a tagged database gives the CSM live ammunition to counter 'are you doing anything for me?' objections, improves retention at renewal, and provides case study material for new sales.
findings.what_they_do: Notion 'wins' database with fields: win type (leads/views/revenue/video performance), client relation, screenshot or media URL, date. Anyone on the team can add a win. KPI: minimum X wins per client per month set as a CSM metric. Wins roll up to client OS so the CSM can open a client record and instantly see a count and list of wins. Used on retention calls, renewals, and sales calls. Goal: ~20+ wins/month company-wide as a tracked number on the client success dashboard.
findings.vs_us: Palm has whale-hunting and Fan Tracker tools (§9) for fan-level data, but no documented system for capturing and storing creator wins (viral posts, subscriber milestones, revenue records) as a retention tool. The §9 gap also notes no written retention strategy. A wins database is directly applicable: Palm's editors and social team observe creator wins daily and could log them systematically.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Creator Wins' table in Airtable (Content Ops or HQ base) with fields: creator, win type (milestone sub count, viral reel, PPV revenue record, press mention), media URL, date. Make logging a win a required step for editors/social team when they observe a notable metric. Josh or a creator success role reviews and shares wins back to creators weekly — this doubles as creator retention messaging and provides case study content for acquisition.

sources.quote: we want to create a culture at our company that we collect wins, we celebrate wins, and we stack wins. We have almost a thousand wins of just screenshots
I would try to get like at least a couple of wins a month for each client and make that like your KPI internally from like a client success perspective
when you're on a call with a client and they're like, 'Oh, I'm just not really seeing anything.' It's like, 'Dude, like we've gotten you 10 leads.'

findings.topic: Role onboarding with daily winning formula checklist
findings.title: Give every new hire a 'daily winning formula' — a repeating Notion task with linked SOPs for each duty
findings.claim: A repeating daily task assigned to each team role, listing every responsibility with embedded Loom SOPs or email templates, eliminates guesswork, prevents the employee from inventing their own process, and lets the founder delegate without micromanaging.
findings.what_they_do: For each role (editor, CSM, appointment setter, etc.): create a Notion task template called '[Role] Daily Winning Formula' that repeats every weekday. Each checklist item links to an SOP (Loom video transcript pasted into Notion) or has a toggle with an exact email template. Example CSM items: 'Maintain daily updates in Notion by 10am', 'Review and QC all video content within 4 hours of submission', 'Send content to clients for approval', 'Update client portal'. Task auto-populates each morning. Pair with a 30-day onboarding course (gallery of Loom recordings) and pre-day-1 through day-6 onboarding checklist.
findings.vs_us: Palm has Clerk roles (editor, ai_editor, chat_manager, creator) and an editor workflow with a day-counter task queue (§1, §10), but no documented daily winning formula, role-specific onboarding checklist, or SOP library linked to recurring tasks. The §10 gap notes no hiring criteria or scaling plan. This tactic is immediately applicable to Palm's editors and Amin (contractor posting role).
findings.recommendation: For Palm's two most active roles — editor and Amin (manual posting) — create a daily Airtable or Notion checklist with 5–8 items each, linked to existing SOPs (or quick Loom recordings). Specifically for the editor: items like 'check task queue, pull next 2 slots, upload to Cloudflare, submit for approval.' For Amin: 'check Telegram topic, post per schedule, reply /posted.' This is a low-effort, high-leverage move to reduce dependency on Josh/Evan for day-to-day direction.

sources.quote: daily winning formula is basically what we'll do with this... I make it a repeating task every weekday... this is a task that just populates every single day for the client success manager
the reason your team would suck is because you're not actually equipping them with things like this to actually do well
every time I found myself doing something repeatedly, I basically just documented it, systematized it using Notion, and then trained someone else to handle it

findings.topic: Loom-to-SOP pipeline for building a scalable SOP library
findings.title: Record a Loom, paste the auto-transcript into ChatGPT, and get a step-by-step SOP in minutes
findings.claim: The fastest way to build an SOP library is to record a Loom of yourself doing the task, copy the auto-generated transcript into ChatGPT with a 'turn this into a step-by-step SOP' prompt, and paste the result directly into a categorized Notion SOP database.
findings.what_they_do: Loom.com recording → copy transcript → ChatGPT prompt → formatted SOP pasted into Notion SOP library, tagged by department (sales, client success, hiring, organic marketing, etc.) and status (draft/active). SOPs are linked from daily winning formula tasks and role onboarding courses via hyperlinks. Cultural rule: when a team member asks a question, don't answer it directly — create an SOP around it, send the SOP, and store it. The SOP library is filtered into role-specific views so each team member sees only their relevant SOPs.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no documented SOP library (§10 gap). The content pipeline (§1) is highly systematized in code and CLAUDE.md but not in a human-readable SOP format accessible to editors or Amin. The Loom-to-SOP method is extremely low-friction and matches Palm's current AI-assisted workflow culture.
findings.recommendation: Start Palm's SOP library with three highest-leverage SOPs: (1) editor submission-to-approval workflow, (2) Amin Telegram posting protocol, (3) creator content filming brief format. Use Loom + ChatGPT as described. Store in Airtable or a Notion page linked from the admin portal. Target: 10 SOPs in 2 weeks, then assign new SOPs as a recurring task whenever a process question is asked more than once.

sources.quote: what we like to do is we like to use a tool called Loom, loom.com, and we just film a video on what we're doing. You go to the video, go to transcript, copy the transcript, turn this into a SOP
anytime somebody asks a question, what we're not doing is we're not just going to answer the question right from the jump. We're just going to create an SOP around it
these are all the SOPs. This is sales, client success, hiring, etc.

findings.title: Structured 5-step onboarding checklist for new creator sign-ons
findings.claim: A repeatable onboarding sequence — contract → account access → OF account audit → marketing setup → expectations meeting — prevents early relationship failures.
findings.what_they_do: After contract signing (via PandaDoc or DocuSign), agency logs into creator's OF account (coordinating 2FA in real time), audits and organizes the subscriber list by spend tier, organizes the content vault, sets up missing social platforms (IG, TikTok, Twitter), sends a content schedule, and holds an expectations-setting conversation covering response windows, platform norms, and month-1/2/3 milestones.
findings.vs_us: Palm has a form-wizard onboarding that captures name, email, commission %, state, contract, and voice memo (§6), but no documented post-signing operational checklist covering OF account audit, vault organization, social setup, or expectations management. The onboarding stops at the contract layer.
findings.recommendation: Extend Palm's onboarding wizard with a post-contract ops checklist: (1) OF login + 2FA coordination, (2) subscriber spend-sort, (3) vault audit/organization, (4) social platform gap-fill, (5) content schedule delivery, (6) expectations call with month-1/2/3 benchmarks. Build this as a checklist state-machine in the Creators table or a dedicated onboarding UI.

sources.quote: first step is to of course do the onboarding right so the onboarding entails a couple of different things

findings.title: Set explicit month-1/2/3 milestones with creator at onboarding
findings.claim: Managing a creator's expectations up front — covering communication channels, response SLAs, and realistic revenue ramps per month — is the single most important onboarding step.
findings.what_they_do: Agency defines and communicates: which platform to message on, guaranteed response windows, and what revenue/subscriber outcomes are realistic in months 1, 2, and 3. They crowdsource realistic benchmarks from a community of 1,000+ operators to give creators honest projections rather than over-promising.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no documented expectations-setting SOP or milestone framework at creator onboarding (§6, §10). There is no written-down month-by-month ramp guide. This is a gap — creator churn risk is highest in the first 90 days when results lag expectations.
findings.recommendation: Create a one-page 'Month 1 / Month 2 / Month 3' benchmark doc (subscriber count, revenue ranges, content volume) derived from Palm's existing creator data. Deliver it at contract signing and reference it in a kickoff call. Log agreed expectations in the Creators table so Josh can track against actuals.

sources.quote: managing expectations so when I say managing expectations that includes when can she message you on what platform what can she expect in the first week in the first month the second month and the third month

findings.title: Match viral inspo accounts to creator's look-type before briefing
findings.claim: Sending creators viral content from models who look like them — same hair color, body type, vibe — dramatically increases the likelihood the creator will replicate it well and that the content will resonate with the right audience.
findings.what_they_do: Agency manually finds a model whose look closely matches the new creator (e.g., 'blonde cheerleader type'), identifies that model's currently-viral videos, and sends them as direct filming references. Creator replicates the format/dance/hook on IG and TikTok.
findings.vs_us: Palm's content pipeline (§1) scrapes inspo from a defined list of accounts and uses AI vision to score virality, but the step of matching inspo sources to a specific creator's look-type is not documented. Inspo may be pooled generically across creators rather than look-matched. This is a refinement opportunity inside the existing pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'look profile' field to the Creators table (hair color, body type, vibe tags). When a new creator onboards, seed their Inspo Sources list with accounts that match their look profile rather than only genre/category. The existing scrape + AI-score pipeline then handles the rest.

sources.quote: try and find her ideas for Content that are similar to her looking so if she's a blond kind of cheerleader type model then fantastic find another model who looks like her and is currently going viral

findings.title: Use US SIM / US-based poster for non-English-speaking creators to avoid low-spend audiences
findings.claim: If a creator posts TikTok from a country with weak purchasing power (e.g., Colombia), viral reach fills her funnel with followers who cannot afford OF subscriptions — a US SIM card or US proxy poster fixes the audience geography.
findings.what_they_do: For creators based in Latin America or other non-Tier-1 countries, agency either ships/provides a US SIM card so the creator's device registers as US-based, or recruits a US-based person to post on her behalf, ensuring TikTok's algorithm surfaces content to US audiences.
findings.vs_us: Palm has a hardware-isolated posting system for AI accounts (§3/§4) with dedicated SIMs per persona, but no documented geo-routing strategy for real creators posting from non-Tier-1 countries. This gap could be costing subscriber conversion rates for any internationally-based Palm creators. (§5 gap)
findings.recommendation: Audit Palm's current real-creator roster for location. For any creator outside Tier-1 English-speaking markets, evaluate whether their TikTok posts are geo-targeting US/UK audiences. If not, explore US SIM provisioning using the existing SIM Inventory infrastructure (§3) or route their posts through Amin with a US-based device.

sources.quote: you would not really my advice would not be for you to get her to post it herself on Tik Tok because you'll if she goes viral she'll get a bunch of Colombian followers and the currency exchange doesn't really make sense

findings.title: Activate Twitter/X early as a high-virality explicit-content funnel
findings.claim: Twitter allows near-explicit content, making it a materially higher-virality channel for OF funnels than IG/TikTok — agencies should set it up at onboarding and teach creators to reply to viral tweets.
findings.what_they_do: Agency sets up Twitter for any creator who lacks it during onboarding, teaches creator to post more explicit (but compliant) content and to reply to trending/viral tweets to capture organic reach. Frames Twitter as a separate traffic channel from IG/TikTok, not a duplicate.
findings.vs_us: Palm's distribution stack is documented around IG/TikTok via Publer and Amin (§5), with Reddit mentioned as a gap but no Twitter/X SOP. Twitter is absent from the documented pipeline. This is a notable traffic gap (§5 🔲 GAP).
findings.recommendation: Add Twitter/X as a standard platform in the social setup checklist for new real-creator onboarding. Build a separate posting cadence for Twitter that allows more explicit previews (within ToS). This is likely a quick win — low setup cost, distinct audience from IG/TikTok, and higher conversion intent.

sources.quote: on Twitter you can get away with more not safeer workor content so AKA showing everything and the chance of that going viral is a lot higher and Twitter's full of that right

findings.topic: Pre-loaded chat scripts with embedded media
findings.title: Equip chatters with pre-written scripts that have photos/videos pre-attached for real-time sends
findings.claim: Chatting drives 80%+ of OF revenue; chatters must have scripts with specific photos, videos, and voice notes pre-loaded so they can send PPV content in real time without hunting the vault mid-conversation.
findings.what_they_do: Agency builds a script library where each conversation branch has the relevant media asset already tagged/attached. Chatters follow the script and send the pre-matched content without interruption, maintaining flow and urgency in the conversation.
findings.vs_us: Palm currently outsources chatting (§8) and has no internal SOP or script library. This is directly relevant build-toward intelligence for Palm's planned in-house chat team (~1yr roadmap) and AI chatting project. The key structural insight is media-to-script pre-linking, not just having scripts alone.
findings.recommendation: When building Palm's in-house chat system, design the script library with media assets pre-linked at the node level (not stored separately). Each chat script node should reference a specific Asset ID from the content vault. This prevents chatters from breaking conversation flow to search the vault — a documented friction point in competitor agencies.

sources.quote: 80% or more should come from the chatting the messages and the tips so because that's the most important part you must have a system in place so you must have set scripts that you or if you've hired somebody to the to do the chatting they can follow now Within These scripts they should have pre-made content already there so photos and videos maybe even some voice notes

findings.title: Sort subscriber list by spend tier immediately upon account access
findings.claim: The first action inside a newly-accessed OF account should be sorting existing subscribers by spend to identify whales and avoid wasting chatter time on low-value fans.
findings.what_they_do: Upon first login to the creator's OF account, agency immediately sorts subscribers by total spend, identifying high-value fans to prioritize in DMs and deprioritizing or ignoring low-spend/no-spend subscribers.
findings.vs_us: Palm has a Whale Hunting module and Fan Tracker CRM (§9) for ongoing fan intelligence, but there is no documented step to perform an initial subscriber spend-sort at the point of account onboarding. The whale-hunting system appears to be an ongoing operational tool rather than an onboarding trigger. Adding this as an explicit Day-1 onboarding action would seed the whale-hunting system immediately.
findings.recommendation: Add 'subscriber spend sort + whale identification' as a mandatory Day-1 step in the creator onboarding checklist. Output should be a list of top-10 spenders imported directly into the Whale Hunting / Fan Tracker tables so the external chat team has prioritized targets from day one of management.

sources.quote: organizing to subscribers seeing who's spent more and who's not organizing them so you don't waste your time

findings.title: Three-platform funnel stack: IG → TikTok → Threads, each with distinct path
findings.claim: Instagram, TikTok, and Threads each require a different funnel path to convert views into paying fans, and together they cover the highest-ROI free traffic sources for AI models.
findings.what_they_do: IG funnel: views → tagged slave account in bio → link in bio → Fanvue → chat → sale. TikTok funnel: views → IG handle in bio (no adult links allowed) → IG bio link → Fanvue. Threads funnel: views → Telegram link in bio → Telegram DM → Fanvue. Each platform's content format and policy dictates the redirect chain.
findings.vs_us: Palm's traffic/distribution strategy is not documented (§5 🔲 GAP). Palm uses Publer + Telegram routing for posting but no written multi-platform funnel architecture exists. Palm's real-creator social pipeline (§1) covers IG/TikTok content production but not the cross-platform redirect logic.
findings.recommendation: Document explicit per-platform funnel paths for both real and AI creators. For real creators, IG bio → Linktree/Beacons → OF is likely already in use informally; codify it. For the AI persona line (§3), adopt the TikTok→IG→OF redirect chain since TikTok blocks adult links directly.

sources.quote: we're only going to go over Instagram, Tik Tok, and threads because all the money I've made so far has been through these three
Funnel one, Instagram. the path they follow, views, tagged account in bio, link in bio, fan view, chatting, and sales

findings.title: IG mother/slave split: main account accumulates reach; disposable slave holds the OF link
findings.claim: Because AI model accounts are vulnerable to Instagram takedowns, you protect compound growth by keeping the main posting account clean and pushing the monetization link through a separate 'slave' account tagged in the bio.
findings.what_they_do: Main (mother) account posts content normally, builds followers and watch time, and tags a second 'slave' account in bio. The slave account holds the link-in-bio to Fanvue/OF. When Instagram removes the slave account (expected), the mother account survives and keeps its audience; a new slave is swapped in. Real human creators avoid this because they're ID-verified and harder to ban.
findings.vs_us: Palm's AI persona warmup system (§3/§4) uses hardware-isolated GrapheneOS devices and aged accounts but does not document a mother/slave split specifically to protect link-in-bio from takedowns. Palm's real-creator accounts would not need this (ID-verified), but AI personas would.
findings.recommendation: For the AI persona line (Amelia, Katie Rosie, Brielle — §3), implement the mother/slave IG architecture: main persona account never holds the OF/Beacons link directly; a linked secondary account absorbs takedown risk. Build a fast slave-account replacement SOP into the warmup playbook (§4).

sources.quote: we're going to take our models on a second account and then put that in bio
This is the mother account, main account, and then the slave account is where we take it at

findings.topic: Threads as underrated early-mover traffic source
findings.title: Threads new-account first-post boost: exploit algorithmic push for fast early traffic
findings.claim: Threads algorithmically amplifies brand-new accounts' first posts, making it faster to gain initial traction than Twitter/X, where momentum takes much longer to build.
findings.what_they_do: Create a new Threads account, post immediately — the first post gets organic reach from Threads' push. Content is simple engaging text (e.g. 'somebody FaceTime me' style posts targeting lonely men). Bio links to Telegram rather than directly to Fanvue, then Telegram DMs are used to warm and convert. Positioned as a 'hidden gem' due to platform newness.
findings.vs_us: Palm's posting/distribution strategy is not documented for Threads (§5 🔲 GAP). Palm uses Publer for IG/TikTok scheduling but Threads is not mentioned. No Telegram-as-intermediate-funnel step is documented at Palm.
findings.recommendation: Test Threads for AI personas immediately while the new-account boost window exists. Start with simple text-based engagement posts. For the AI persona line, use the Telegram intermediate step (persona Telegram → Fanvue) since it adds a warm pre-sell layer before the paid platform. Evaluate adding Threads to Publer scheduling (Phase 3 — §5).

sources.quote: Threads is really underrated. It can bring in a lot of traffic especially right now because it's more of a newer platform
when you make a brand new Threads account or get a Threads account right off the bat, your first post is going to get views

findings.title: Threads 30-slave-account follow-bot: 1,200 targeted leads/day via viral-post follower targeting
findings.claim: Running 30+ Threads slave accounts that each follow 40–50 recent likers of viral similar posts generates ~1,200 targeted daily leads who see the main account funnel.
findings.what_they_do: One main Threads account posts daily content with Fanvue link. 30–50 slave accounts each have bio text 'I'm more active here' tagging the main. A custom Chrome extension bot finds recently viral posts in the niche, filters likes by recency, and auto-follows those users (40–50 per slave per session). Notified users see a profile → visit main → subscribe. AdsPower browser manages all slave accounts. The bot is distributed via private Discord.
findings.vs_us: Palm has no documented Threads strategy or follower-growth automation (§5 🔲 GAP). Palm does use Publer for scheduling but not engagement automation. This level of account farming on Threads is outside Palm's current documented ops.
findings.recommendation: For the AI persona scaling line (§3), evaluate the Threads slave-account follow strategy as a traffic lever. The math (1,200 leads/day) is compelling if sustainable. Before adopting: assess Threads TOS risk (similar to IG follow-loop risks) and whether AdsPower fits alongside existing GrapheneOS/hardware isolation setup (§4). Start with a small 5-account pilot before scaling to 30+.

sources.quote: You need 30 50 of these. They just have to post and in the bar you can say I'm more active here and tag your main account
30 slave accounts, multiply by 40 follows, 1,200 targeted leads a day, you will get subscribers

findings.topic: Platform-native content formats for AI model social
findings.title: Format-match by platform: long-caption storytelling on IG, slideshows on TikTok, text posts on Threads
findings.claim: Each platform rewards a specific content format; matching format to platform algorithm dramatically increases reach and watch time for AI model accounts.
findings.what_they_do: Instagram: long-caption 'storytelling' posts where the persona narrates a relatable story (ex-drama etc.) — increases watch time which triggers broader algorithmic push; captions generated via ChatGPT from real OFI model examples. Also 'caption hook videos' with an on-screen text hook. TikTok: slideshow format prioritized because TikTok is actively promoting slides and they go viral more easily; targets lonely-men content angles. Threads: short punchy text posts ('somebody FaceTime me') that feel real and prompt engagement from the target demographic.
findings.vs_us: Palm's content engine (§1) is strong at inspo-scraping and replicating viral Reels but is built around video for IG. The Airtable pipeline does not appear to have a Threads text-post workflow or TikTok slideshow format lane. Content format strategy is not documented per-platform.
findings.recommendation: Extend Palm's content pipeline (§1) to include platform-specific format lanes: (1) TikTok slideshow template workflow, (2) Threads text-post templates. For real creators, these formats can supplement the existing Reels-first approach. For AI personas, add ChatGPT-assisted long-caption storytelling as a content type in the `Inspiration`/`Assets` pipeline.

sources.quote: Find some good storytelling post from OFI models on IG and use chat GTP to make your own
when it comes to Tik Tok you're going to want to do more slides because Tik Tok really favors slides

findings.topic: Telegram as pre-sell warm layer before paid platform
findings.title: Telegram intermediate funnel: capture Threads leads into Telegram before converting to Fanvue
findings.claim: Routing Threads traffic through a persona Telegram account before the paid platform creates a warmer, higher-converting lead because men have already self-selected into a direct-message relationship.
findings.what_they_do: Threads bio links to a Telegram account (not directly to Fanvue). Men on Threads already use Telegram, so friction is low. The chatter engages them in Telegram DMs to build rapport, then funnels them to Fanvue subscription. This pre-sell layer increases conversion versus cold-clicking a paid platform link.
findings.vs_us: Palm's chatting is currently outsourced (§8) with no internal SOP for pre-platform warm-up funnels. Palm's Beacons link-in-bio goes directly to OF. A Telegram intermediate step is not part of Palm's documented funnel. This is build-toward intelligence for the in-house chat team roadmap.
findings.recommendation: When building the in-house chat team (§8 roadmap), design the funnel to include a Telegram warm layer for traffic sources where direct OF links are blocked or cold (Threads, TikTok). Document a Telegram-to-OF conversion script as part of the chatter SOP. Test with AI persona accounts first before rolling to real creators.

sources.quote: you're going to put in your Telegram link in the bio. Make a Telegram account for your model and then put that in the bio for a threads
a lot of these men have Telegram so they'll hit you up on Telegram and then from there direct them funnel them into your fan view
